sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Apple to cut iPhone, AirPods output, Nikkei reports
feed.summary: Apple Inc. plans to reduce iPhone SE and AirPods production due to a slowdown in demand caused by the Ukraine crisis and rising inflation, according to a Nikkei report. The company intends to produce 20% fewer iPhone SEs next quarter and has cut 2022 orders for AirPods by over 10 million units. Analysts attribute the weaker demand to COVID-19 lockdowns in China, inflation, and the lack of significant design upgrades for the latest iPhone SE model.

feed.title: Amazon Stock Rally Erases Losses for the Year
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares rallied, becoming the first megacap tech stock to recover its losses for the year,

feed.title: WA Police phone capabilities expanded to add support for Apple CarPlay
feed.summary: WA Police's phone capabilities are expanding to include Apple CarPlay support for frontline officers. This initiative, part of a $34 million McGowan Government investment, allows officers to access police systems and real-time incident information directly from their car's display, enhancing safety and operational efficiency. WA Police is reportedly the first law enforcement agency globally to adopt Apple CarPlay for policing activities.

feed.title: Intel CEO earned 1,711 times more than average company worker in 2021
feed.summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger earned $178.6 million in 2021, which is 1,711 times more than the average Intel employee's salary and 698% higher than his predecessor's pay. Most of Gelsinger's compensation was in stock awards, including one-time new-hire equity. This disclosure comes as Intel asks shareholders to approve executive compensation at their upcoming annual meeting, while executive pay across the U.S. continues to rise.

feed.title: Companies to reimburse Idaho workers for travel costs to get abortion in another state
feed.summary: Two companies with employees in Idaho, Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Citigroup Inc., announced they will cover travel expenses for workers seeking abortions in other states. This decision comes as a highly restrictive abortion law is set to take effect in Idaho on April 22nd. Other major companies, including Apple, Levi Strauss & Co., Match Group, and Bumble Inc., are also offering similar benefits to employees in states with restrictive abortion laws.

feed.title: Warren Buffett's 3 Greatest Investments of All Time
feed.summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has a remarkable track record for identifying highly profitable investments. This article highlights his three greatest investments: Apple (AAPL) for its massive nominal dollar return, GEICO for its stellar return as an acquisition, and Moody's (MCO) for its impressive percentage gain from an existing investment. These choices underscore Buffett's strategic approach to value creation through both direct stock holdings and company acquisitions.

feed.title: SPAC Tied to Trump’s Social Media Venture Sinks as Downloads Slump 95%
feed.summary: The SPAC Digital World Acquisition Corp., which is slated to merge with Trump Media & Technology Group, has seen its stock sink after the Truth Social app's daily downloads plummeted by 95% since its launch. The app's user growth has been slow, partly due to technical issues and its unavailability on Android devices, leading to investor concern despite an initial surge in the SPAC's shares.

feed.title: Apple reportedly seeks alternative flash memory chip suppliers
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly looking into adding China's Yangtze Memory Technologies Corp. as a supplier for NAND flash memory chips. This move comes after a supply chain disruption at a facility co-owned by Kioxia Corp. and Western Digital Corp., which are key flash memory suppliers for Apple. The potential partnership with Yangtze Memory aims to diversify Apple's supply chain and reduce reliance on existing suppliers, despite geopolitical considerations and Yangtze Memory's older technology compared to competitors.

feed.title: Why Big Companies Like Tesla and Amazon Are Splitting Stocks
feed.summary: This article explains what a stock split is and why major companies like Tesla, Amazon, and Alphabet execute them. It delves into the financial and psychological reasons behind stock splits, such as making shares more accessible to a wider range of investors and signaling positive future performance, despite the total market capitalization remaining unchanged. The piece also discusses the impact of stock splits on retail investors and highlights the strong post-split performance of such stocks.

feed.title: Putnam’s Collins Draws On Nature For ESG Investing Inspiration
feed.summary: Katherine Collins, head of sustainable investing at Putnam Investments, integrates biomimicry and insights from nature, specifically honeybees, into her ESG investment strategies. After a career in traditional finance and a master's degree from Harvard Divinity School, Collins applies a unique lens to identify companies demonstrating leadership in sustainability. Her approach focuses on balancing risk and opportunity, evidenced in her co-management of Putnam Sustainable Leaders (PNOYX) and Putnam Sustainable Future (PMVYX) funds.

feed.title: BlackBerry plans to settle lawsuit over BlackBerry 10, avoid U.S. trial
feed.summary: BlackBerry Ltd plans to settle an eight-year-old class-action lawsuit alleging that it defrauded shareholders by misrepresenting the success of its BlackBerry 10 smartphones. A federal judge in Manhattan granted a request to adjourn the planned trial to allow for settlement negotiations. The lawsuit claims BlackBerry concealed the device's true sales prospects, which led to an inflated share price before the company ceased phone manufacturing in 2016 to pivot to cybersecurity.

feed.title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway reveals major stake in HP, tech stock soars
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has acquired a significant stake in HP Inc., becoming the largest shareholder in the PC and printer manufacturer. This investment, valued at approximately $4.2 billion, caused HP's stock to surge by 14.7%. The move signals a renewed period of activity for Berkshire Hathaway and adds HP to Buffett's select portfolio of tech holdings.

feed.title: House Republicans want to axe Citi contracts over bank's abortion benefits
feed.summary: House Republicans are calling for the cancellation of government contracts with Citigroup after the banking giant announced it would cover travel costs for employees seeking abortions. Republican Rep. Mike Johnson and 44 other lawmakers argue that continuing the contract would violate the policy of not funding abortions with taxpayer money. This controversy highlights the increasing focus on companies' stances on political and social issues, with other corporations also facing scrutiny for their policies on reproductive healthcare and other contentious matters.

feed.title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Reveals $4 Billion Stake in HP
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. has acquired an over 11% stake in computer and printer manufacturer HP Inc., valued at approximately $4.2 billion. This investment marks another significant move into the technology sector for the once tech-averse billionaire investor. The share purchases were disclosed through securities filings on Wednesday.

feed.title: Is QQQ A Good Buy For Dividend Portfolios? Why There Are Better Choices
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust, Series 1 ETF (QQQ) has seen strong capital appreciation in recent years, largely due to factors like ultra-loose monetary policy and pandemic tailwinds. However, these factors are unlikely to persist, making QQQ less suitable for income-focused dividend portfolios. The author suggests investors might find better income opportunities by selecting individual stocks.

feed.title: Warren Buffett made about $650 million on HP after stake was revealed
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway saw its stake in HP increase by about $650 million to $4.85 billion after the company revealed its ownership of 121 million shares. HP's stock surged 15% following the disclosure, marking its best day in over two years. This swift market reaction highlights the continued influence of Buffett's investment decisions on investor behavior.

feed.title: Dreams of green data
feed.summary: This article discusses the efforts by companies like Apple, Google, and Equinix, alongside Santa Clara officials, to make data centers more environmentally friendly. The primary focus is on reducing energy consumption and shifting towards carbon emission-free power sources for these energy-intensive technological backbones. The article highlights the increasing importance of cleaner power sources and less electricity use for data centers.

feed.title: Survey finds 87% of US teenagers own an iPhone, Apple's AirPods remain immensely popular
feed.summary: A Piper Sandler survey reveals that 87% of US teenagers own an iPhone and 72% own AirPods, demonstrating Apple's continued dominance in these product categories among this demographic. The Apple Watch also saw growth, now owned by 37% of teens, and Apple Pay is preferred for contactless payments. However, Apple TV+ lags behind Netflix and YouTube for daily media consumption.

feed.title: Evolution of GIS: How Maps Have Progressed from a Mapping Pancake to a Soufflé
feed.summary: The article discusses the evolution of Geospatial Information Systems (GIS) from rudimentary mapping to sophisticated platforms integral to daily life and various industries. It highlights how digital maps and smartphone integration have transformed navigation and planning, expanding GIS applications beyond traditional uses to include military, agriculture, retail, and logistics. The piece also addresses challenges in real-time data and information authenticity while forecasting future trends, including AI integration, disaster management, and smart city applications.

feed.title: Harsh truth: Trump’s social media app follows long line of failed ventures
feed.summary: Donald Trump's social media platform, Truth Social, has experienced significant challenges since its launch, with low engagement, declining downloads, and executive resignations. Despite Trump's high expectations for the app as an alternative to "big tech," it has struggled to gain traction and user interest, with many users stuck on waitlists and Trump himself rarely using the platform. These issues, coupled with investigations into the SPAC that intends to take it public, raise questions about Truth Social's future, especially as Elon Musk's new involvement with Twitter hints at a potential reinstatement of Trump's account there.

feed.title: DeFiChain Adds New dTokens Corresponding to Disney, Intel, MicroStrategy, and iShares MSCI China ETF
feed.summary: DeFiChain has announced the addition of four new decentralized tokens, or dTokens, following a community vote. These new dTokens correspond to Walt Disney Co ($dDIS), iShares MSCI China ETF ($dMCHI), MicroStrategy Incorporated ($dMSTR), and Intel Corporation ($dINTC), allowing users to gain price exposure to these traditional assets without ownership. DeFiChain, a blockchain built on the Bitcoin network, aims to provide decentralized financial applications and services, expanding its dToken universe to offer an alternative to traditional financial brokers.

feed.title: LIVEONE RECOGNIZED AS ONE OF 'THE BEST ANDROID MUSIC APPS FOR 2022' BY PC MAG
feed.summary: LiveOne (Nasdaq: LVO) has been recognized by PC Mag as one of "The Best Android Music Apps for 2022," placing it alongside major streaming services like Amazon Music and Spotify. This marks a continued validation for LiveOne, which PC Mag previously called one of the "most complete streaming music services" and awarded its Editors' Choice. The recognition highlights LiveOne's curated channels, smart DJ hosts, and overall slick design as key factors in its appeal.

feed.title: Apple starts manufacturing iPhone 13 in India
feed.summary: Apple Inc has begun manufacturing its iPhone 13 model in India, aiming to reduce its reliance on Chinese supply chains. The production is taking place at Foxconn's plant in Sriperumbudur, Tamil Nadu, marking the fourth iPhone model to be produced locally in India since 2017. This move aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify its production to markets like India, Mexico, and Vietnam.

feed.title: Sony, Lego Owner Invest $2B in ‘Fortnite’ Publisher Epic Games
feed.summary: Sony Group Corp. and KIRKBI, the owner of Lego, have invested a combined $2 billion in Epic Games, the publisher of Fortnite and creator of the Unreal Engine. This investment boosts Epic's valuation to $31.5 billion and is intended to accelerate the company's efforts to build the metaverse. Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney stated the funds will help create new social entertainment experiences exploring the connection between digital and physical worlds.

feed.title: The Biggest Reveals from Bitcoin 2022
feed.summary: The Bitcoin 2022 conference in Miami highlighted the early stage of Bitcoin adoption, with comparisons drawn to the internet in 1995. Key announcements included Robinhood rolling out crypto wallets and Lightning Network integration, new jurisdictions like Prospera (Honduras) and Madeira adopting Bitcoin as legal tender, and Strike partnering with major point-of-sale systems like Shopify and McDonald's to facilitate Bitcoin payments. The article emphasizes Bitcoin's potential for mass adoption and its disruptive impact on payment processing.

feed.title: Snapchat Lawsuit Claims Executives Kept Investors In Dark About New Apple Privacy Restrictions
feed.summary: A derivative lawsuit alleges that Snapchat executives, including CEO Evan Spiegel and CFO Derek Andersen, concealed from investors the negative impact Apple's new privacy features would have on Snap's advertising revenue. The lawsuit claims executives downplayed the effects, leading to a 26% drop in Snap's shares after a weaker-than-expected October 2021 earnings report. Plaintiff Vonmarie Thomas is seeking declaratory relief and restitution to Snap Inc. for alleged insider trading, unjust enrichment, and breach of fiduciary duties.

feed.title: After Effects on Apple M1: giving motion designers the speed they need
feed.summary: Adobe After Effects now offers native support for Apple M1, significantly boosting performance for motion designers, making it up to three times faster on M1 Ultra Macs. The latest release also introduces integrated review and approval tools with Frame.io for Creative Cloud and Scene Edit Detection, powered by Adobe Sensei, for more efficient workflow. These updates aim to enhance creative flexibility and speed up project turnaround times for users.

feed.title: 13 must-have accessories for your new Peloton Guide
feed.summary: This article lists 13 essential accessories for new Peloton Guide users, including dumbbells, workout mats, heart rate monitors, fans, recovery tools, and supplements. It provides specific product recommendations and explanations for how each item can enhance the Peloton Guide workout experience. The goal is to help users maximize results from their strength training with the Peloton Guide.

feed.title: Yelp Joins Apple, Citigroup, Other Companies Covering Travel Costs for Abortions
feed.summary: Yelp Inc. announced it would provide financial assistance to employees and their dependents for out-of-state travel costs related to abortion care, expanding their health insurance benefits. This move follows similar actions by companies like Apple and Citigroup as more states introduce restrictive abortion laws. The expansion aims to support employees seeking reproductive healthcare in states where access is more readily available.

feed.title: Futu SG (moomoo), the 1st global digital brokerage, offers $0 platform fee*, $0 market data fee, lifetime $0 commission** on US stocks as markets rally despite conflicts and rising inflation - PR Newswire APAC
feed.summary: Futu Singapore (moomoo) is offering a lifetime $0 commission on US stock trading, along with $0 platform and market data fees, to attract investors amidst market volatility. Data from their platform shows an increasing interest in US stocks among Singaporean investors, particularly those aged 40 and above, with technology and growth stocks being popular choices. The initiative aims to provide enhanced access to US markets and support investors with educational resources and community features.

feed.title: Rare PFAS factory shutdown in Belgium costs 3M
feed.summary: 3M is experiencing a significant disruption in sales due to the government-ordered temporary closure of its PFAS production plant in Zwijndrecht, Belgium. This closure, which has lasted five months, has particularly impacted the semiconductor, data center, and automotive industries, as the Belgian facility is a major producer of electronics coolants. The company is working to meet new, stricter PFAS emissions rules in Belgium and has pledged over $300 million for environmental remediation related to historical PFAS contamination in the region.

feed.title: This Chip Maker Supplies Tesla and Apple. Its Stock Could Get a Boost as Shortages Continue.
feed.summary: Dutch-Swiss chip maker STMicroelectronics has experienced a boom in business due to increasing global demand and persistent supply constraints. The company supplies chips to major tech firms like Tesla and Apple. Its stock is projected to benefit from the ongoing semiconductor shortages in the market.

feed.title: Why did Buffett buy HP Inc (and should you invest too)?
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently acquired an 11.4% stake in HP Inc., making it Berkshire's second-largest tech position after Apple. This investment comes amidst concerns about inflation and the PC market, yet HP's strong fundamentals, consistent cash flow, strategic expansion into gaming and remote work solutions, and attractive valuation at a low P/E ratio and a 2.6% dividend yield, make it an appealing "boring but dependable cash machine" for value investors. The article questions whether individual investors should follow Buffett, advising personal research and considering pullbacks before investing.

feed.title: Apple helps suppliers rapidly accelerate renewable energy use around the world
feed.summary: Apple announced that its suppliers more than doubled their use of clean power over the last year, with over 10 gigawatts operational out of nearly 16 gigawatts committed. 213 major manufacturing partners have pledged to power all Apple production with renewable electricity across 25 countries, accelerating progress toward Apple’s 2030 carbon neutral goal. Apple is also investing directly in renewable energy projects globally and supporting communities through its Power for Impact program.

feed.title: Apple, others face shipment delays as China COVID curbs squeeze suppliers
feed.summary: Shipments of Apple products, Dell, and Lenovo laptops are facing potential delays due to China's ongoing COVID-19 lockdowns, which have forced suppliers like Pegatron and Quanta Computer to suspend operations. The impact's severity depends on the lockdown duration, with a longer lockdown potentially causing significant shortages for end-users, despite some companies' efforts to reroute production or use alternative plants.

feed.title: TikTok owner ByteDance challenges Tencent, NetEase in China’s music-streaming market
feed.summary: ByteDance, the owner of TikTok and Douyin, has launched a new music-streaming app in China called Qishui Yinyue ("soda music") to compete with market leaders Tencent Music Entertainment Group and NetEase. The app, which is currently in beta, offers over 10 million tracks and uses ByteDance's personalization algorithms to recommend music, similar to its overseas app Resso. This move signifies ByteDance's direct challenge to existing dominant players in the Chinese music-streaming market.

feed.title: The Chips That Rebooted the Mac
feed.summary: Apple successfully revitalized its Mac computer line by designing its own M1 chips, replacing Intel processors. This strategic move, led by Johny Srouji, improved Mac performance, energy efficiency, and profitability, and helped Apple navigate supply-chain disruptions. The in-house chip design capability also positions Apple for future products like cars and extended-reality headsets.

feed.title: Netflix Scores as California Judge Says Broadcom’s Dynamic Resource Provisioning Patent Claim is Abstract under Alice
feed.summary: A U.S. District Judge in California has invalidated a Broadcom patent claim against Netflix, ruling it was an abstract idea under the Alice/Mayo framework. The patent, related to dynamic resource provisioning for job placement, was deemed no different from common task allocation, dealing a setback to Broadcom's patent enforcement campaign against Netflix. This decision is part of ongoing litigation where Broadcom asserts Netflix's streaming services infringe on its server technology patents, affecting Broadcom's traditional set-top box business.

feed.title: Why we may not get a Garmin Vivoactive 5: rumours, leaks, expectations
feed.summary: This article explores the possibility that Garmin may not release a Vivoactive 5, suggesting the company might be phasing out the line in favor of its Venu series due to display similarities and Venu's improved battery life. It also discusses potential design and feature expectations for a hypothetical Vivoactive 5, including enhanced training metrics, new sensors like ECG and thermometers, and cellular connectivity. The author suggests that if no Vivoactive 5 is released by September of that year, the line might be discontinued.

feed.title: LinkedIn was the most exploited brand in phishing attacks last quarter
feed.summary: LinkedIn became the most exploited brand in phishing attacks during Q1 2022, accounting for 52% of all analyzed attempts, a significant jump from 8% in the previous quarter. Cyber threat intelligence provider Check Point Research reported this surge, noting that phishers are increasingly leveraging social networks. The article details examples of these attacks, including spoofed LinkedIn and Maersk emails, and offers tips to avoid falling victim to phishing.

feed.title: Spotify review: The most popular music service needs to fix some serious issues
feed.summary: This review of Spotify highlights its excellent recommendation algorithm and social features but criticizes its "unacceptably low streaming quality" compared to competitors that offer lossless audio. The article notes Spotify's failure to deliver on its promised "HiFi" tier and discusses controversies surrounding its royalty payments to artists and investments outside of music. It concludes that while Spotify excels in music discovery, its lagging audio quality and other issues mean many users might find a better listening experience elsewhere.

feed.title: Netflix loses 200,000 subscribers in first quarter
feed.summary: Netflix reported a loss of 200,000 subscribers in the first quarter, missing its own projections and marking its first decline in over a decade. The company attributes the subscriber loss to password sharing, increased competition from other streaming services, and geopolitical events. Netflix is exploring new strategies to regain subscribers, including a lower-priced ad-supported service and cracking down on password sharing.

feed.title: Netflix Loses $54 Billion in Market Cap After Biggest One-Day Stock Drop Ever
feed.summary: Netflix experienced its biggest one-day stock drop ever, losing $54 billion in market capitalization after announcing its first subscriber loss in over a decade and projecting further losses. Analysts downgraded the stock, citing market saturation, increased competition, and the impact of account sharing, leading to a significant reassessment of the company's growth prospects.

feed.title: Netflix shares slide after it loses 200,000 subscribers
feed.summary: Netflix shares plummeted after the company reported losing 200,000 subscribers in the first quarter of 2022, its first subscriber loss in over a decade. The decline was attributed to increased competition, economic headwinds, market saturation, and the withdrawal from Russia, leading to a significant drop in stock price and concerns about future growth. Netflix plans to address the issue by cracking down on password sharing and exploring new revenue streams like video games.

feed.title: Apple Spends Record High on Lobbying Amid Pressure from Congress
feed.summary: Apple Inc. spent a record $2.5 million on lobbying in the first quarter of 2022, marking a 34% increase from the previous quarter. This significant expenditure comes as the company faces growing pressure from the U.S. Congress regarding antitrust concerns. Other major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Microsoft are also heavily investing in lobbying efforts as Congress considers antitrust legislation that could impact the technology sector.

feed.title: International Business Machines Corp. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
feed.summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) shares rose 1.11% to $139.85, outperforming a generally down market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both fell. This marks the third consecutive day of gains for IBM, which also outperformed competitors like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet. The company closed $6.14 below its 52-week high, and its trading volume was more than double its 50-day average.

feed.title: Leaked Chats Show LAPSUS$ Stole T-Mobile Source Code – Krebs on Security
feed.summary: Leaked private chat messages from the LAPSUS$ cybercrime group reveal they breached T-Mobile multiple times in March 2022, stealing source code for various projects. Despite T-Mobile's claim that no customer or government information was compromised, the chats detail LAPSUS$'s methods, including purchasing access, social engineering employees, and utilizing internal tools like Atlas to look up sensitive accounts. The article also highlights the group's internal conflicts, their leader's fixation on stealing source code, and their eventual undoing due to compromised servers and arrests.

feed.title: Review: Was Steve Jobs the soul of Apple Inc.? Reporter says yes and charts the aftermath
feed.summary: This article reviews Tripp Mickle's book, "After Steve: How Apple Became a Trillion-Dollar Company and Lost Its Soul," which argues that Apple under Tim Cook has become less innovative but significantly richer, focusing on services and China deals rather than groundbreaking new devices. The review highlights Mickle's dual portrait of Tim Cook and Jony Ive, exploring their different impacts on Apple's trajectory post-Steve Jobs. While acknowledging the book's thorough character studies, the reviewer criticizes its narrow focus on these executives, which neglects broader ethical considerations and societal impacts of Apple's ubiquitous products.

feed.title: Stock tied to Donald Trump's social media venture falls 13% as Twitter announces deal with Musk
feed.summary: Shares of Digital World Acquisition Corp. (DWAC), the SPAC set to merge with Donald Trump's media venture, fell 12.9% after Twitter announced it would be acquired by Elon Musk. The dip is attributed to Musk's stated intention to change Twitter's censorship policies, potentially reducing the need for alternative platforms like Truth Social. Despite its volatile nature, DWAC's stock has more than tripled since its September launch at $10.

feed.title: What is fintech and why does it matter?
feed.summary: Fintech, a combination of “financial” and “technology,” refers to software that makes financial processes more accessible and automates traditional financial services. Kirsty Rutter, Fintech Investment Director at Lloyds Banking Group, explains how fintech is transforming banking by improving customer experiences through technologies like AI and machine learning. Lloyds Banking Group is actively investing in and collaborating with fintech companies to enhance its services and maintain a competitive edge.

feed.title: Apple planning to spend $19.3 million upgrading MetLife building in Cary
feed.summary: Apple is planning to invest $19.3 million to upgrade a MetLife building in Cary, one year after announcing its new headquarters in Research Triangle Park. The renovations primarily involve interior work on floors 2-6 and 1,7 while the company recruits technical staff and engineers. This development is expected to significantly impact the local job market, housing, and bring further economic and philanthropic engagement to the Triangle region.

feed.title: Low Volatility Stocks Usually Do Well—But There Are Traps
feed.summary: A Northern Trust Asset Management paper warns that while low-volatility stocks generally outperform over time, they can present traps, particularly in real estate, utilities, and consumer staples sectors. Rising interest rates and economic vulnerability can negatively impact these types of low-volatility holdings. NTAM advises focusing on companies with strong cash flows, profitability, and solid balance sheets to mitigate these risks.

feed.title: Apple plans Cary office project to fill seven-story tower
feed.summary: One year after announcing a new headquarters in Research Triangle Park, Apple is now planning a significant renovation project in Cary. The company intends to spend approximately $19.3 million to renovate the seven-floor MetLife Building 3 at 301 MetLife Way. This move indicates Apple's ongoing expansion and investment in the region.

feed.title: Funds That Buy Like Buffett, 2022
feed.summary: This article identifies mutual funds that invest similarly to Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway. It highlights funds that hold a high percentage of the same stocks as Buffett's portfolio, with a particular focus on Apple. The piece also discusses the investment strategies of these funds and their performance.

feed.title: Crescent Hotels & Resorts Adds Aloft Mountain View to Latitudes Collection
feed.summary: Crescent Hotels & Resorts has expanded its Latitudes Collection with the addition of the Aloft Mountain View. This property, formerly a Hilton Garden Inn, underwent extensive renovations and is designed to attract energetic business travelers with its vibrant, tech-savvy atmosphere and modern amenities. Located near major tech companies in Silicon Valley, the hotel offers a 24/7 fitness center, an outdoor heated pool, event space, and on-site dining options.

feed.title: LG Display quarterly profit plunges as demand shrinks and prices slide
feed.summary: LG Display Co Ltd reported a 93% plunge in first-quarter operating profit, far below forecasts, due to shrinking demand for TVs, smartphones, and laptops, and fierce competition leading to declining panel prices. The company announced a strategic shift towards higher-end products and a reduction in the production of commoditized LCD TV panels, where Chinese rivals have a competitive edge. This downturn was exacerbated by China's COVID-19 lockdowns, which caused supply chain disruptions.

feed.title: Warren Buffett and Berkshire Hathaway are once again outperforming the stock market. Apple is one big reason but these other 10 stocks also helped
feed.summary: Warren Buffett and Berkshire Hathaway are once again outperforming the stock market, with Apple being a significant contributor. This article highlights Berkshire's excellent performance in 2022, contrasting it with the broader market's struggles. It also suggests that the idea of Buffett's investing style being outdated is itself becoming outdated.

feed.title: Fidelity Enters the Metaverse with New Financial Education Experience: Invest Quest at The Fidelity Stack
feed.summary: Fidelity Investments has launched a new gamified financial education experience called "The Fidelity Stack" in Decentraland, an eight-story virtual building designed to teach users about ETF investing through an "Invest Quest" challenge. This initiative aims to engage younger audiences, particularly the 18-35 age range, following the company's recent introduction of a metaverse-themed ETF, FMET. Fidelity views this as a significant step in providing financial education in decentralized, virtual communities.

feed.title: Governing Platforms Through Apple’s App Store in the U.S. and China
feed.summary: This article examines how Apple's App Store is governed in the U.S. and China, highlighting similarities in regulatory tools despite different political systems. It argues that while both countries use similar policies, the key difference lies in the ability of private actors and civil society to challenge government decisions, which is significantly weaker in China. The piece concludes by conceptualizing China's approach as "institutional governance outsourcing" due to commercial incentives, economic embeddedness, and differing values that compel Apple to comply more readily with Chinese demands.

feed.title: What Elon Musk’s Twitter Takeover Means for Trump’s TRUTH Social
feed.summary: Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter has sparked debate about its implications for former President Donald Trump's TRUTH Social. While some conservatives on Twitter celebrated the deal and anticipate the return of suspended accounts, Trump has stated he will not rejoin Twitter, opting instead to focus on TRUTH Social. The deal initially led to a surge in TRUTH Social downloads but also introduced uncertainty about its long-term growth and financial stability due to renewed interest in Twitter and the complexities of its SPAC merger.

feed.title: Tencent Music Adds International Distribution Option For Artists
feed.summary: Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) has launched "One-Click For All," a new global distribution service allowing artists to distribute music to over 150 international platforms like Apple Music, YouTube, and Spotify. This move comes as TME faces increased competition from ByteDance's Qishui Yinyue and NetEase Cloud Music in China, with the latter also suing TME for alleged unfair competition and copyright infringement. The service offers participating artists free ISRCs and up to 70% of their income after service fees, bolstering TME's presence in the international music market.

feed.title: Advisors Are Powering Portfolios With Thematic ETFs
feed.summary: Thematic ETFs, which invest in companies poised to benefit from long-term transformative changes, are increasingly being adopted by financial advisors as a replacement for traditional sector funds. These ETFs offer more precise exposure to growth trends like blockchain, emergent food, robotics, and electric vehicles. Asset managers are expanding their thematic ETF offerings, with BlackRock (iShares) recently launching two new products in this space, highlighting a growing conviction in the long-term benefits of thematic investing despite some recent market volatility.

feed.title: Apple sees bigger supply problems after strong start to year
feed.summary: Apple Inc. anticipates significant supply chain disruptions in the upcoming fiscal quarter, primarily due to COVID-19 lockdowns in China and ongoing chip shortages, which are expected to reduce sales by $4 billion to $8 billion. Despite reporting record profits and sales for its fiscal second quarter, the company's shares dropped following the glum outlook. The war in Ukraine and slowing growth in services also contribute to the forecast difficulties, although demand for iPhones has remained strong.

feed.title: Nasdaq 100 Index: Tech Stocks Lose $1.8 Trillion in a Month Thanks to Fed
feed.summary: Tech investors have experienced the worst start to the year in two decades, with the Nasdaq 100 Index losing over $1.8 trillion in value in April alone. This decline is attributed to a tumultuous earnings season, fears of an economic slowdown, and aggressive expectations regarding the Federal Reserve's rate-hike trajectory, with another hike anticipated next week. Investors are bracing for continued volatility in the market.

feed.title: Amazon’s biggest drop since 2006 caps dismal month for tech
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares experienced their largest one-day drop since 2006, sinking 14% and erasing $206.2 billion from its market value, following a disappointing outlook. This slump contributed to a dismal April for major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Tesla, which collectively lost a trillion dollars in market value due to tumultuous earnings, rising costs, and concerns over Federal Reserve policy. The Nasdaq 100 Index also saw its biggest one-month decline since October 2008.

feed.title: Sonos is now part of the Matter smart home alliance, joining Amazon, Apple and Google
feed.summary: Sonos has joined the Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA), the group behind the Matter smart home standard, which aims to create a unified protocol for interoperability between smart home devices from various manufacturers. While Sonos has a history of protecting its intellectual property, the company stated its commitment to ensuring its products integrate seamlessly with other smart home solutions. Although its involvement is in early stages, this move signals Sonos's interest in the growing smart home ecosystem, potentially influencing future product developments.

feed.title: Apple Sales Top Estimates on Strong iPhone and Services Demand
feed.summary: Apple Inc. shares dipped after the company predicted significant revenue loss due to supply constraints in the current quarter, despite reporting record-setting results for the previous fiscal period. The company expects the constraints, stemming from Covid restrictions in China, to cost between $4 billion and $8 billion, even as strong demand for iPhones and services fueled sales and profit above analyst estimates. Apple also announced a new $90 billion stock buyback program.

feed.title: Top 10 Growth Stocks by Market Capitalization
feed.summary: This article defines growth stocks and explains how they differ from other investment types, highlighting their potential for capital appreciation and typical reinvestment strategies. It then presents a list of the top 10 large-cap growth stocks as of April 2022, detailing their market capitalization, forward P/E ratio, dividend status, and specific growth drivers for each company. The article also touches on general market performance of growth stocks and factors influencing their volatility.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting
feed.summary: Reporters Steve Jordon and Cindy Gonzalez provide a live report from Berkshire Hathaway’s 2022 annual meeting, held on Saturday, April 30, at the CHI Health Center convention center in downtown Omaha. Warren Buffett discussed Berkshire Hathaway as a painting with an "ever-expanding canvas" and shared comments on inflation, foreign businesses, and the U.S. currency. Charlie Munger also weighed in on topics like China investments and cryptocurrency.

feed.title: Mass-Market Selfie Drones are Here: Snapchat's New Pixy
feed.summary: Snap Inc. has launched Pixy, a mass-market selfie drone designed to be user-friendly with a simple interface and toy-like appearance, making it accessible to a wider audience. Priced at $250, Pixy offers four pre-set flight patterns and transfers footage wirelessly to Snapchat for editing and sharing. This innovation is expected to make the selfie stick obsolete by offering an easier and more advanced way to take photos and videos.

feed.title: Apple lawsuit says 'stealth' startup Rivos poached engineers to steal secrets
feed.summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against "stealth" startup Rivos Inc., alleging that Rivos stole its computer-chip trade secrets after poaching over 40 Apple engineers. The lawsuit claims that at least two former Apple engineers took gigabytes of confidential information related to Apple's system-on-chip (SoC) technology with them to Rivos. Apple is seeking to prevent Rivos from using its trade secrets and is asking for undisclosed money damages.

feed.title: Apple hit with EU antitrust charge over mobile payments technology
feed.summary: The European Union has issued an antitrust charge against Apple, alleging that the company restricts rivals' access to its mobile payments technology, Apple Pay, used on iOS devices. This action follows a previous EU charge related to the music streaming market. Apple could face a substantial fine, potentially up to 10% of its global turnover, and may be forced to open its NFC technology to competing mobile wallet solutions.

feed.title: Garmin Epix Gen 2 Review: One Of The Finest Premium Smartwatches Money Can Buy
feed.summary: The Garmin Epix Gen 2 is a high-end smartwatch designed for serious fitness enthusiasts, featuring a vibrant AMOLED display and robust fitness tracking capabilities. While it comes with a premium price of around Rs 1 lakh and lacks some lifestyle features like call answering or message replies, its battery life, durability, and accurate health metrics make it a top contender in the premium fitness smartwatch market. The reviewer notes that despite a few downsides, its overall performance solidifies its position as one of the best premium smartwatches available.

feed.title: With interest rates rising, it's time to focus on MANG stocks instead of FAANG, according to Jefferies
feed.summary: Jefferies analysts recommend focusing on "MANG" stocks (Microsoft, Apple, Nvidia, and Alphabet) over "FAANG + Microsoft" as interest rates rise and the Federal Reserve tightens monetary policy. This shift is presented as a safer investment strategy during an environment of increasing rates. The article highlights the rationale behind prioritizing this particular group of tech giants.

feed.title: With interest rates rising, it's time to focus on MANG stocks instead of FAANG, according to Jefferies
feed.summary: Jefferies analysts recommend focusing on "MANG" stocks (Microsoft, Apple, Nvidia, and Alphabet) instead of the traditional "FAANG + Microsoft" group as interest rates rise. They suggest that MANG stocks present a safer investment strategy during the Federal Reserve's monetary policy tightening. This advice was issued prior to a Federal Open Market Committee meeting.

feed.title: Enovix Announces Initial Smartwatch Battery Order from Leading Global Consumer Electronics Company
feed.summary: Enovix Corporation (Nasdaq: ENVX) announced receipt of an initial order for its high energy density smartwatch battery from a leading global consumer electronics company. This order follows a successful technology validation and marks the beginning of the customer's product development process, potentially leading to the integration of Enovix batteries into next-generation smartwatches and high-volume production. The batteries will be produced at Enovix's Fab-1 facility in Fremont, California.

feed.title: Stock Splits Lead to Split Results in Trading
feed.summary: This article analyzes the impact of stock splits on market structure, liquidity, retail investor participation, notional exposure, trade size, odd lots, and volatility in both equities and options markets. Using data from 61 stock splits since 2020, including a case study on Apple (AAPL), the Cboe team found that stock splits do not necessarily increase liquidity proportionally to the split ratio and can lead to a decrease in overall investor capital exposure, though they do increase retail investor participation. The study suggests while splits enhance accessibility, they may not always benefit existing shareholders.

feed.title: FMET Is a New, Fabulous Metaverse Idea
feed.summary: Fidelity has launched the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), which tracks the Fidelity Metaverse Index, to capitalize on the nascent but expanding metaverse investment theme. The ETF mixes established tech giants with innovative smaller companies, including Meta Platforms, Apple, NVIDIA, Tencent, Baidu, and JD.com. Investors are advised that patience will be key for metaverse investing due to anticipated volatility, though the long-term growth outlook is considered bright with billions in projected revenue.

feed.title: Stock Price Trading Dynamics and Splits
feed.summary: This article examines the impact of stock price on market quality features and the decision to split a stock, focusing on the recent trend of large corporations splitting their high-priced stocks. It analyzes various market features like consolidated quoted spreads, quoted size coverage, and quote volatility across different stock price and volume categories. The analysis suggests that targeting stock prices in the $50-$100 range for liquid securities offers the best market quality results, although the evidence for splitting less liquid stocks is less convincing.

feed.title: Tech sell-off pushes Nasdaq to steepest drop since June 2020
feed.summary: A tech sell-off on Thursday, May 5, 2022 pushed the Nasdaq Composite to its steepest drop since June 2020, wiping out hundreds of billions in market value. This downturn was driven by disappointing earnings reports from cloud and e-commerce companies like Shopify, Amazon, and Meta, coupled with investor concerns following the Federal Reserve's interest rate hike to combat inflation. The rotation out of tech began in late 2021 due to inflation fears and escalated with events like Russia's invasion of Ukraine, leading to significant year-to-date losses for many "Covid winner" stocks.

feed.title: The Old Tech FAANG Is Dulled, But a New Version Has Promise
feed.summary: Bank of America strategists have introduced "FAANG 2.0," a new grouping of sectors designed for a world facing inflation, war, and geopolitical shifts, replacing the underperforming original tech FAANG stocks. This new version, comprising fuels, aerospace and defense, agriculture, nuclear and renewables, and gold and metals/minerals, has shown significantly better market performance year to date compared to both the S&P 500 and the original tech FAANG stocks. The article highlights the rationale behind this shift, citing energy security, increased military spending, rising food prices, and gold's role as a refuge asset in the current global climate.

feed.title: Class Action Complaint Claims Apple, Google, Snap Liable for Facilitating 'Sexual Grooming' of Minors
feed.summary: Apple, Google, and Snap Inc. are facing a class action lawsuit in California Southern District Court for allegedly enabling the sexual grooming of minors. The suit stems from a criminal case where Snapchat was used to coerce a minor into pornographic acts. The plaintiffs seek to compel the defendants to enforce policies to prevent such criminal conduct and comply with child abuse laws.

feed.title: Korean Webtoon Companies that are giving the Audience Addictive Content
feed.summary: The Korean webtoon industry is experiencing significant global growth, driven by the success of K-culture, despite challenges like increased competition and a decline in artists' average income in 2023. Key players like Lezhin Comics, Naver's Webtoon and Line Manga, KakaoPage, Ridi Corp's Manta, Toomics, and Tappytoon are expanding internationally, leveraging diverse genres, free-reading models, and innovative technologies like AI and NFTs. These companies are not only captivating worldwide audiences but also transforming the global entertainment landscape through partnerships and strategic investments.

feed.title: Meta is opening its first store as VR headsets inch closer to mainstream reality
feed.summary: Meta Platforms is opening its first physical store to sell VR headsets like the Oculus Quest 2, despite its Reality Labs division facing significant losses. The company is betting heavily on the metaverse, viewing it as the solution to boost virtual reality adoption beyond the current low engagement among teens. Experts suggest that widespread VR adoption could be triggered by one or two "killer applications" and that Meta's strategy is to build the foundational platform for this new computing paradigm, even if profitability remains years away.

feed.title: 3 Surefire Stocks That Can Turn $200,000 Into $1 Million by 2030
feed.summary: This article identifies three stocks — Nio, Upstart Holdings, and Pinterest — that have the potential to turn a $200,000 investment into $1 million by 2030, despite recent market downturns. The author emphasizes that while the stock market has experienced corrections, these companies possess strong catalysts and innovative business models that position them for significant long-term growth. The piece details how each company, through unique offerings and market positioning, is poised for substantial returns within the next eight years.

feed.title: Vietnam 'darling' of global electronics giants
feed.summary: Vietnam has emerged as a leading hub for electronics manufacturing in Southeast Asia, attracting significant investments from global giants like Samsung, LG, Intel, and Apple suppliers such as Foxconn, Pegatron, Goertek, and Luxshare. The country's electronics exports have become a key earner, with major manufacturers expanding operations and establishing R&D centers, contributing to substantial economic growth and technological advancement.

feed.title: 15 Top Cities for Machine Learning Engineer Salaries
feed.summary: This article identifies the top 15 U.S. cities offering the highest salaries for Machine Learning Engineers, based on data from Blind. It highlights that major tech hubs like Silicon Valley and Seattle lead due to high demand from companies like Meta and Apple. The piece also notes the increasing importance and compensation for AI and machine learning skills across industries.

feed.title: Digital Health Literacy: Why It’s Important and How to Improve It
feed.summary: As healthcare increasingly relies on technology, digital health literacy is crucial for ensuring health equity. This article defines digital health literacy, explains the consequences of its absence, and outlines strategies for healthcare providers to assess and improve patients' ability to use digital health tools and evaluate online health information. It emphasizes the need for comprehensive plans to ensure all patients can benefit from modern patient engagement technologies.

feed.title: Former Senior Vice President of Autonomous Driving at VW Group Alexander Hitzinger Joins Arbe Board of Directors
feed.summary: Alexander Hitzinger, former Senior Vice President of Autonomous Driving at VW Group, has joined Arbe's Board of Directors. Hitzinger brings extensive experience from top automotive and new mobility firms, including Volkswagen and Apple, to Arbe. His addition is expected to help Arbe revolutionize the radar industry and advance safe autonomous driving solutions.

feed.title: Tinder owner Match sues Google in 'last resort' to avoid app-store booting
feed.summary: Match Group Inc., owner of dating apps like Tinder, has sued Alphabet Inc.'s Google, describing it as a "last resort" to prevent its apps from being removed from the Google Play store. Match is contesting Google's policy of demanding up to 30% of sales from apps and threatening to block downloads unless Match uses Google's payment system exclusively. The lawsuit accuses Google of violating antitrust laws and seeks to protect Match from potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue payments to Google.

feed.title: Telesat, Globalstar, Intelsat headline upheavals in satellite industry
feed.summary: The satellite communications industry is undergoing significant changes, with major providers like Telesat, Globalstar, and Intelsat experiencing corporate upheavals, new leadership, and project adjustments. These developments underscore the evolving role of satellites in global telecommunications, moving from a relatively minor component to a potentially prominent one due to innovations from companies like OneWeb and SpaceX. These shifts also highlight a broader trend where various companies, including large telecom providers, are

feed.title: How Citizens Financial used an iPhone plan to create a nationwide digital bank
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group Inc. launched its Citizens Pay iPhone upgrade plan in 2015, an early iteration of the buy-now-pay-later model. This initiative allowed Citizens to leverage its brand as a credit provider in conjunction with Apple's iPhone, helping customers bypass long waits for new iPhone releases. The article explores how this strategy contributes to Citizens' nationwide digital banking efforts.

feed.title: Online dating giant Match Group sues Google over app store
feed.summary: Match Group, the company behind popular dating apps like Tinder and OkCupid, has sued Google, alleging an illegal monopoly in the Google Play Store and anticompetitive in-app payment policies. Match Group claims Google's 30% cut of in-app purchases and its mandate to use Google's payment technology are harmful, especially as Google threatened to remove Match apps from the Play Store if they don't comply with new rules. Google, in response, argues Match Group is trying to avoid paying for services and that the majority of apps on its platform do not pay fees.

feed.title: Grand Investment Opportunity Awaits in the Metaverse
feed.summary: Despite the current downturn in growth and technology stocks, the metaverse offers a significant long-term investment opportunity. The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) is presented as a viable option for investors who wish to capitalize on this emerging concept without individual stock picking, holding familiar large-cap tech companies. Experts believe the metaverse has the potential to transform social media, gaming, shopping, entertainment, and education.

feed.title: The First Truly Personal Computer
feed.summary: M.G. Siegler reflects on the discontinuance of the iPod Touch, marking the end of the entire iPod line. He reminisces about his first Apple product being an iPod in 2004 and the device's significance in Apple's growth towards becoming a multi-trillion dollar company. Siegler expresses a nostalgic wish for a new, simple, music-focused iPod, while acknowledging Apple's current ecosystem of iPhone, Apple Watch, and AirPods serves a similar purpose.

feed.title: Unity Software loses $5 billion in market cap after Apple's changes lead to 'self-inflicted wound'
feed.summary: Unity Software's market cap dropped by $5 billion after the company's attempts to circumvent Apple's IDFA changes backfired, leading to a "self-inflicted wound." Wall Street punished Unity U stock, slashing price targets by 43% as their workaround negatively impacted ad accuracy. Unity executives' strategy concerning Apple's mobile operating system changes proved ineffective, resulting in a significant market downturn for the company.

feed.title: Saudi Aramco Becomes World’s Most Valuable Stock as Apple Drops
feed.summary: Saudi Aramco has surpassed Apple Inc. to become the world's most valuable company for the first time since 2020. This shift is attributed to surging oil prices benefiting the crude producer, while inflation and technology stock weakness have led to a decline in Apple's valuation. Aramco's market capitalization reached approximately $2.43 trillion, exceeding Apple's $2.37 trillion.

feed.title: Apple defeats new trial bid after overturning $506 mln patent verdict
feed.summary: A U.S. federal court has rejected the University of Wisconsin's patent-licensing arm, WARF, bid for a new trial against Apple Inc. This decision follows the Supreme Court's annulment of a previous $506 million patent infringement award against Apple. WARF had sought to reopen the case with a new theory of infringement, but the court ruled against it, issuing a final judgment for Apple.

feed.title: Home Audio Equipment Market size to Increase by USD 14.39 Bn | Featuring Top Vendors Including Apple Inc., Bose Corp., Koninklijke Philips NV, and Kripa Electronics India Pvt. Ltd. | Technavio
feed.summary: The global home audio equipment market is projected to grow by USD 14.39 billion between 2020 and 2025, exhibiting a CAGR of 10.76%. This growth is primarily driven by rising disposable incomes and technological innovations, especially in the smart speakers segment. Key vendors include Apple Inc., Bose Corp., Koninklijke Philips NV, and others.

feed.title: Breaking Bad's Giancarlo Esposito Is The Voice Of Sonos' New Alexa-Style Assistant
feed.summary: Sonos has announced a new voice control feature called Sonos Voice Control, launching June 1 in the US. This Alexa-style assistant will be voiced by Giancarlo Esposito, known for his roles in "Breaking Bad" and "The Mandalorian." The company emphasizes that user requests are processed locally and not sent to the cloud, ensuring privacy.

feed.title: A customer-centric approach: the key to the innovation paradox
feed.summary: Kavitha Mariappan, a woman leader in tech, emphasizes that diversity and resilience are crucial for innovation. She highlights the innovation paradox, explaining that while customer centricity is vital, innovators must go beyond direct customer input to truly innovate, as customers often think incrementally. The key is to involve customers throughout the product development journey, focusing on their behaviors, use cases, and desired outcomes, and challenging them to envision future possibilities, which has been central to Zscaler's success.

feed.title: Domino’s grants ‘Stranger Things’ fans ‘mind-ordering’ powers through new app
feed.summary: Domino's has launched a "mind-ordering" app in promotion of season four of Netflix's "Stranger Things," allowing users to order pizza using facial recognition and eye-tracking technology. The app lets fans role-play as characters from the show and explore a virtual Hawkins National Laboratory. While Domino's says no personally identifiable facial data is stored, the initiative aims to experiment with nascent tech while engaging "Stranger Things" fans and boosting sales amid recent declines and inflationary pressures.

feed.title: Starbucks to cover employees’ travel expenses for abortions; gender surgeries
feed.summary: Starbucks announced an expansion of its health care benefits to cover travel expenses for employees seeking abortions or gender-affirming procedures if these services are not available within 100 miles of their homes. This decision comes in the wake of a leaked draft U.S. Supreme Court opinion potentially overturning Roe v. Wade, with the company affirming its commitment to ensuring employee access to quality healthcare. Starbucks joins other major companies in offering this benefit, consistent with its history of advocating for liberal causes.

feed.title: SK Telecom's latest virtual assistant is more than just a voice
feed.summary: SK Telecom has launched "A.", a new virtual assistant app that differentiates itself from others like Siri and Bixby by featuring a customizable character. This app aims to be a personalized daily companion for users, offering content curation, schedule management, and conversational capabilities, while integrating various services and utilizing the GPT-3 language model.

feed.title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Acquires Small Stake in Paramount Global
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested approximately $2.6 billion in Paramount Global during the first quarter of 2022, acquiring nearly 15% of the company's Class B shares. This move signals Buffett's confidence in Paramount Global's valuation and its efforts in the competitive streaming market with Paramount+, alongside its other media properties like CBS and MTV. Berkshire Hathaway also made significant investments in other companies like Chevron and Apple, while reducing stakes in Kroger and Verizon, and selling off Abbvie and Bristol Myers Squibb.

feed.title: Cashing in on the pandemic: the five highest-paid US executives in 2021
feed.summary: A Wall Street Journal analysis revealed that the highest-paid S&P 500 executives saw record compensation in 2021, with a median of $14.7 million, largely due to stock-based pay capitalizing on the pandemic recovery. Expedia CEO Peter Kern topped the list, earning $296 million primarily in shares and options, followed by David Zaslav of Discovery, William McDermott of ServiceNow, Tim Cook of Apple, and Jamie Dimon of JP Morgan, whose compensations were also heavily tied to stock performance and incentives. This surge in executive pay reflects a strong benefit from the post-pandemic stock market rebound.

feed.title: Competition Intensifying in LTPO OLED Display Market
feed.summary: Competition in the mobile OLED panel market, particularly in the LTPO OLED segment, is intensifying as LG Display and BOE rapidly increase production. While Samsung Display currently leads, BOE is expanding its capacity to supply Apple's iPhone 15, and Apple is also partnering with LG Display for LTPO panels and plans to use them in future iPads and MacBooks. This has led to other companies like Visionox and CSOT also entering mass production of LTPO OLEDs.

feed.title: Virtual Carriers Beat AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon on Customer Satisfaction
feed.summary: A survey by the American Customer Satisfaction Index (ACSI) found that virtual wireless carriers like Consumer Cellular and Straight Talk received higher customer satisfaction scores than major carriers such as AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon. This finding aligns with PCMag's own Readers' Choice Awards, suggesting that customers are happier with Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs) that lease network access from the larger providers. Despite the big three performing similarly in sub-ratings, MVNOs consistently outrank them in overall customer satisfaction.

feed.title: SEIV ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns CBOE:SEIV
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the SEI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Value Factor ETF (SEIV), focusing on its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It highlights SEIV as an actively managed fund targeting US large-cap value stocks using a quantitative-based selection process. The analysis covers AUM, expense ratio, holdings breakdown by sector and region, and dividend history, including a 24.49% increase in its last reported dividend.

feed.title: HP Envy 34 All-in-One Desktop review: A big-screen AIO for power users and creators
feed.summary: HP has updated its Envy All-in-One desktop, featuring a 34-inch 5K wide-screen display, making it a strong alternative to the discontinued 27-inch iMac for creative users and power users. This review highlights its impressive design, versatile features including accessible RAM/SSD slots, and strong performance, especially in graphics, though it notes some bloatware and a lack of display swivel. The Envy 34 offers various configurations, from an entry-level model to a high-end option with a Core i9 processor and RTX 3080 graphics.

feed.title: Apple introduces new professional training to support growing IT workforce
feed.summary: Apple has launched updated professional training and certifications for IT support and management, delivered through new online, self-paced courses. These certifications, including Apple Certified Support Professional and Apple Certified IT Professional, aim to address the growing demand for IT professionals skilled in supporting Apple products within enterprises. Apple is also partnering with community colleges, universities, and the Mac Admins Foundation to provide scholarships and broad access to these new training programs.

feed.title: US stocks suffer biggest daily drop in almost two years
feed.summary: US stocks experienced their largest daily decline in nearly two years, as inflation concerns and the prospect of stricter monetary policy weighed on markets. The S&P 500 dropped 4%, with consumer shares plummeting over 6%, led by Target's steep fall after a reduced profit forecast. Growing hawkish sentiment from the Federal Reserve, global supply chain issues, and geopolitical tensions continue to fuel investor anxiety.

feed.title: Why S&P booted Tesla from its ESG index
feed.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices removed Tesla from its S&P 500 ESG Index due to a decline in its ESG rank compared to global peers. Despite Tesla's role in the transition to electric vehicles, the decision was influenced by claims of racial discrimination, poor working conditions at its factory, and its handling of a federal investigation into autopilot-related deaths. Elon Musk called ESG an "outrageous scam" in response to the decision.

feed.title: Robert Downey Jr. Transforms Classic Cars Into Eco-Friendly Green Machines in New Series Coming to discovery+
feed.summary: Robert Downey Jr. is set to star in a new discovery+ original series titled "Downey's Dream Cars" (wt), launching later in 2022. In the show, Downey and a team of experts will restore six classic cars from his personal collection, updating them with eco-friendly technologies while preserving their original style. This project combines his passion for climate change solutions with classic car restoration, challenging viewers to find modern inspiration in vintage automobiles.

feed.title: How to Send Money: The 9 Best Ways to Transfer Money
feed.summary: This article details nine of the best ways to send money, catering to various needs from instant peer-to-peer transfers to large international sums. It compares options like Zelle, Venmo, Cash App, wire transfers, and money orders, highlighting their fees, transfer limits, and transfer times. The guide aims to help users choose the most suitable method for their specific financial transactions.

feed.title: With multi-device FIDO credentials, you can now go all-in on passwordless | CXO Revolutionaries
feed.summary: Apple, Google, and Microsoft's expanded support for multi-device FIDO credentials is set to revolutionize passwordless logins, addressing previous limitations of FIDO authentication like device-locked cryptographic keys and complex re-enrollment processes. This development, coupled with the Federal Zero Trust Strategy requiring phishing-resistant authentication, positions FIDO to become a prevalent, high-assurance authentication method for both consumers and federal use cases. The update promises seamless and secure access to FIDO credentials across multiple devices, even after device replacement.

feed.title: GameStop Corp. Cl A stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: GameStop Corp. Cl A (GME) stock rallied 8.43% on Thursday, closing at $99.19, despite a generally dismal trading session for the broader stock market. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw declines. GameStop outperformed competitors like Apple, AT&T, and Best Buy, though its trading volume remained below its 50-day average. The stock was $245.47 short of its 52-week high.

feed.title: SEI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Value Factor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:SEIV)
feed.summary: The SEI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Value Factor ETF (SEIV) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund that invests in U.S. large-cap stocks perceived to be undervalued. Launched on May 18, 2022, and managed by SEI, SEIV has assets under management of $1.35 billion and a net expense ratio of 0.15%. As of the article's date, SEIV shares were trading at $45.25, reflecting a 9.0% increase year-to-date, and its top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Apple.

feed.title: AC Hotels by Marriott developer buys land in St. Johns Town Center
feed.summary: OTO Development LLC, the developer behind the AC Hotels by Marriott project in St. Johns Town Center, has purchased 2.725 acres of land for $1.18 million. The four-story, 118-room hotel, costing an estimated $18 million, is scheduled to start construction in the first quarter of 2022 and be completed in the second quarter of 2023. This project is a joint venture between OTO and Simon Property Group, with OTO serving as the managing partner.

feed.title: Semiconductor Competition: Opportunities for Taiwan
feed.summary: The article discusses how Taiwan, particularly TSMC, dominates the global semiconductor industry despite intensifying geopolitical competition and efforts by countries like the US, EU, and China to achieve self-sufficiency in chip manufacturing. It highlights Taiwan's current technological edge and suggests that rather than threats, these global shifts present opportunities for Taiwanese firms to expand into new markets, enhance R&D, and diversify their economic reliance on manufacturing through strategic international partnerships.

feed.title: International Business Machines Corp. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) stock fell 0.91% to $128.48 on Friday, May 20, 2022, underperforming the broader stock market, which saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rise slightly. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for IBM, leaving its stock $17.51 below its 52-week high. While IBM declined, some competitors showed mixed performance, with Apple Inc. (AAPL) rising and Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and Alphabet Inc. Cl A (GOOGL) falling.

feed.title: Read This Before You Buy Any Udemy Course
feed.summary: This article provides essential tips for purchasing courses on Udemy, emphasizing avoiding impulse buys due to frequent discounts and supporting instructors by using their direct links. It also advises against coupon sites and recommends buying courses via a desktop browser in incognito mode for the best pricing.

feed.title: Amazon (AMZN) Announces New Fire 7 Tablets
feed.summary: Amazon has announced its new Fire 7 and Fire 7 Kids tablets, which are available for pre-order and will start shipping on June 29, 2022. These tablets serve as budget-friendly alternatives to the Apple iPad mini, with prices starting at $59.99 and $109.99 respectively. The new Fire 7 boasts significant upgrades in battery life, RAM, and responsiveness compared to its 2019 predecessor, while only increasing in price by $10.

feed.title: Cleveland-Cliffs and TravelCenters of America return to Fortune 500
feed.summary: Cleveland-Cliffs and TravelCenters of America have rejoined the Fortune 500 list. Walmart topped the overall list, followed by Amazon and Apple. The article also mentions other related business news.

feed.title: Broadcom Inc in talks to acquire VMware: sources
feed.summary: Broadcom Inc. is reportedly in discussions to acquire cloud-computing company VMware Inc., a deal that would significantly expand the chipmaker's software presence. This potential acquisition aligns with CEO Hock Tan's strategy of diversifying Broadcom through software companies. VMware, valued at around US$40 billion, would provide Broadcom with greater access to the data center market, a critical area for growth.

feed.title: Apple's Steve Jobs: An Extraordinary Career
feed.summary: This article chronicles the extraordinary career of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, highlighting how his vision propelled the PC revolution and Apple's success. It covers his early life, his initial departure from Apple, his ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, where he spearheaded groundbreaking products like the iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad. The piece concludes with his health struggles, resignation, and passing, emphasizing his lasting legacy as a visionary leader.

feed.title: Where Does the Supply Chain Crisis Stand Now?
feed.summary: The article discusses the ongoing global supply chain crisis, examining its causes, current impact across various sectors like semiconductors, automobiles, industrials, retail, and restaurants, and potential timelines for recovery. It highlights that while some improvements are being seen, new bottlenecks, such as the war in Ukraine and lockdowns in China, continue to pose challenges, pushing companies to adapt strategies like diversifying supply chains and securing long-term contracts.

feed.title: Newport plant critical to easing microchip shortage, says boss
feed.summary: A factory in Newport, Wales, run by IQE, is highlighted as critical to addressing the global semiconductor shortage. New chief executive Americo Lemos is seeking government and private funding to expand the facility, which produces essential components for various electronic devices, including iPhones. The Welsh government supports the expansion, emphasizing the need for a coherent UK approach to the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: 5 lessons I learned from helping to launch Tesla’s Model S
feed.summary: The article shares five business lessons learned by Sukemasa Kabayama, the former President of Tesla Japan, during the challenging five-week launch of the Model S in Japan in 2014. These lessons emphasize creativity, holistic customer experience, laser focus on outcomes, the principle of "less is more," and clarity in problem-solving. Kabayama also draws parallels to his current startup, Uplift Labs.

feed.title: Deere Models Apple to Drive Revenue with Autonomy
feed.summary: Deere & Co. is adopting a strategy similar to Apple's, combining its machinery with software and subscription models to boost revenue, focusing on autonomy and AI in agriculture. The company plans to sell autonomy as a separate, likely subscription-based service for its $500,000 autonomous 8R tractor, with experts suggesting this recurring revenue model could significantly improve profit margins. Deere's move comes after a recent stock drop and aims to capitalize on market needs like labor shortages and high input costs by offering efficiency through data-driven precision agriculture.

feed.title: Micron, Netflix, and 5 Other Beaten-Up Tech Stocks That Could Be Bargains
feed.summary: This article identifies seven technology stocks that have experienced significant drops in value during the current year but could offer attractive investment opportunities. It notes that while the broader U.S. stock market has faced challenges, high-growth technology stocks have been particularly hard hit. The piece specifically mentions Micron and Netflix as examples of these beaten-up tech stocks.

feed.title: How Chatbots are Revolutionizing Customer Experience
feed.summary: Modern AI-powered chatbots are transforming customer experience by handling various tasks from ordering taxis to booking appointments, driven by advancements in AI, Machine Learning, and Natural Language Processing (NLP). Unlike traditional rule-based bots, NLP allows chatbots to understand complex conversations, retain context, and interpret human sentiment by integrating with external knowledge sources. Conversational AI, built on neural networks, enables chatbots to learn and improve continuously, process vast amounts of unstructured data to predict user behavior, and perform repetitive tasks, making them indispensable for businesses and freeing up human employees for more complex issues.

feed.title: Apple can't dodge rival app store Cydia's antitrust lawsuit
feed.summary: A California federal judge has allowed rival app store Cydia's antitrust lawsuit against Apple Inc. to proceed, rejecting Apple's argument that the claims were time-barred. The lawsuit accuses Apple of maintaining an unlawful monopoly over software distribution on its iOS operating system and that its technological updates between 2018 and 2021 harmed app distributors like Cydia. This case is similar to the antitrust suit brought by Epic Games against Apple.

feed.title: Mobile Mondays: SanDisk iXpand Flash Drive Luxe provides ease of mind with convenient transfer and backup
feed.summary: The SanDisk iXpand Flash Drive Luxe offers a convenient solution for transferring and backing up files between iOS devices, computers, and tablets thanks to its dual Lightning and USB-C connectors. While its read and write speeds are notably slow, it excels as a hassle-free backup device for iPhone photos, eliminating the need for cables or adapters. The review highlights its portability and ease of use, despite the slower performance, making it a practical tool for organizing and securing digital content.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
feed.summary: Apple Inc.'s stock (AAPL) fell 0.53% to $148.84 on Tuesday, underperforming the broader market as the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declined. This marked the end of a three-day winning streak for Apple, and the stock closed $34.10 below its 52-week high. Competitors showed mixed results, with Microsoft (MSFT) and IBM (IBM) also falling, while Alphabet (GOOGL) saw a gain.

feed.title: Diablo Immortal Review
feed.summary: This extensive review of Diablo Immortal dives into Blizzard's free-to-play mobile MMO action-RPG, highlighting its core Diablo elements despite its mobile platform and microtransaction systems. The author praises its depth, storyline, and optimization, while noting it might not be welcoming for new Diablo players. Despite initial skepticism about mobile games and monetization, the review concludes that Diablo Immortal is shaping up to be one of the best mobile offerings available.

feed.title: Apple to improve working hours for retail staff after union push
feed.summary: Apple is implementing more flexible work schedules for its retail staff in response to unionization efforts and employee frustrations. Changes include longer breaks between shifts, limits on late-night work, fewer consecutive workdays, and guaranteed weekend days off for full-time employees. These adjustments are part of broader efforts by Apple to improve working conditions and address labor market challenges.

feed.title: Block to integrate Square payments with Apple iPhone's 'Tap to Pay' feature
feed.summary: Block Inc (SQ.N) announced on Thursday that it will integrate Square payments with Apple's "Tap to Pay" feature on iPhones, planning to launch the service later this year. This integration will allow sellers to accept contactless payments using only their iPhone and the Square Point-of-Sales app, eliminating the need for additional hardware. Apple (AAPL.O) previously stated that its "Tap to Pay" feature would debut this year, with other companies like Stripe and Shopify also offering the service.

feed.title: What Can a Multi-Factor Screening Methodology Do for Your Core Equity Exposure?
feed.summary: This article discusses how applying a multi-factor screening methodology, focusing on value, quality, and momentum, can enhance core equity exposure for large-cap stock portfolios. It highlights research from FlexShares suggesting that this approach can deliver superior risk-adjusted returns compared to the broad large-cap market. The FlexShares US Quality Large Cap Index Fund (QLC) is presented as an example of an ETF that utilizes these factors.

feed.title: What Can a Multi-Factor Screening Methodology Do for Your Core Equity Exposure?
feed.summary: This article discusses how applying a multi-factor screening methodology can improve risk-adjusted returns for large-cap equity portfolios. It highlights factors such as value, quality, and momentum, and introduces the FlexShares US Quality Large Cap Index Fund (QLC) which integrates these factors.
