The fund aims to provide benefits of these factor exposures while maintaining a similar risk profile to the broader large-cap market.

feed.title: Here are this year’s Fortune 500 companies by region
feed.summary: The 2022 Fortune 500 companies collectively generated $16.1 trillion in revenue, reflecting a 19% increase from 2020. This article breaks down the geographical distribution of these companies across the U.S. and highlights dominant industries in the Western, Midwest, Northeast, and Southern regions. Insights into key companies and financial performances specific to each region are also provided.

feed.title: Motley Fool: A logistics leader
feed.summary: GXO Logistics, the world's largest pure-play contract logistics company, reported strong first-quarter results with 14% revenue growth and doubling net income, despite a general downturn in e-commerce stocks. The company, which operates high-tech warehouses for major multinational clients, is strategically positioned to gain market share even in a recession due to its "cost-plus" contracts and investments in areas like reverse logistics. The article also includes "Ask the Fool" sections addressing hyperinflation, investment strategies, and a "dumbest investment" anecdote regarding Black Elk Energy bonds.

feed.title: WWDC22 highlights
feed.summary: Apple kicked off its annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC22) with a special event at Apple Park, welcoming developers, students, and media. The event showcased the redesigned MacBook Air and updated 13-inch MacBook Pro with the M2 chip, alongside new features for iOS 16, iPadOS 16, macOS Ventura, and watchOS 9. The conference also unveiled the new Apple Developer Center and provided access to over 175 developer sessions.

feed.title: Apple announces winners of the 2022 Apple Design Awards
feed.summary: Apple announced the winners of its 2022 Apple Design Awards, celebrating 12 outstanding apps and games selected from 36 finalists from around the world. These winners were recognized across six categories: Inclusivity, Delight and Fun, Interaction, Social Impact, Visuals and Graphics, and Innovation, for their creativity, innovation, and exceptional design. The awards highlight how developers leverage Apple technologies to create transformative and engaging user experiences.

feed.title: watchOS 9 delivers new ways to stay connected, active, and healthy
feed.summary: Apple previewed watchOS 9, introducing customizable watch faces, an enhanced Workout app with advanced metrics and new training experiences, and sleep stages. A first-of-its-kind FDA-cleared AFib History feature offers deeper health insights, and a new Medications app simplifies tracking and managing prescriptions. These updates aim to provide users with scientifically validated insights across fitness, sleep, and heart health, while enhancing personalization options for their Apple Watch.

feed.title: iPadOS 16 takes the versatility of iPad even further with powerful new productivity and collaboration features
feed.summary: Apple previewed iPadOS 16, introducing a new multitasking experience with Stage Manager and external display support, enhanced collaboration through Messages and a new Freeform app, and significant updates to Mail and Safari. The update also brings pro features like Reference Mode and Display Zoom, the Weather app, and improved Live Text, making the iPad even more versatile and powerful, especially for M1 chip-equipped devices. These features aim to boost productivity, collaboration, and introduce new ways to interact with content.

feed.title: Apple's iOS 16 update will let you edit and unsend text messages
feed.summary: Apple's iOS 16 update introduces the ability for users to edit and unsend text messages within a 15-minute window. This feature, along with the option to recover deleted messages and mark conversations as unread, was announced during Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference. The update also brings a personalized lock screen with customizable date and time, and the addition of widgets for quick access to information.

feed.title: With shares of Kansas City’s Cerner turned over, sale to Oracle will close Wednesday
feed.summary: Oracle's acquisition of Cerner Corporation for $28.3 billion is set to formally close on Wednesday, June 8th, following a successful stock tender offer. This acquisition marks Oracle's largest ever and signifies its entry into the healthcare market, with Cerner continuing as a dedicated subsidiary and Oracle pledging to grow jobs in Kansas City. The deal concludes Cerner's status as the largest private employer under local control in Kansas City, a company founded in 1979.

feed.title: A wholly owned subsidiary of Apple will extend loans for its Pay Later service
feed.summary: Apple's new Pay Later service will have a wholly owned subsidiary handle credit checks and short-term loans, signaling the company's deeper push into financial services. While Apple is partnering with Mastercard and Goldman Sachs, it will manage the lending decisions and balance sheet itself. This move aims to make the iPhone ecosystem more valuable to users and is part of Apple's strategy to bring fintech infrastructure in-house.

feed.title: Baidu's electric vehicle firm Jidu unveils first 'robot' car
feed.summary: Baidu's EV arm Jidu Auto has unveiled its first "robot" concept car, aiming for mass production in 2023. This vehicle, co-funded by Chinese automaker Geely, features Level 4 autonomous capabilities and voice recognition, and is designed to attract tech-savvy users. The move positions Baidu among global tech companies developing smart cars following Tesla's success.

feed.title: Apple Will Handle Lending Itself With New Pay Later Service
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is set to manage its own lending operations for a new "buy now, pay later" service called Apple Pay Later, rather than relying on third-party partners. A subsidiary, Apple Financing LLC, will conduct credit checks and make loan decisions, holding the necessary state lending licenses. This move signifies Apple's deeper push into the financial services sector.

feed.title: New American Fortune 500 in 2022: The Largest American Companies and Their Immigrant Roots
feed.summary: A report from the American Immigration Council finds that 43.8% of the 2022 Fortune 500 companies were founded by immigrants or their children. These "New American" firms generated over $7 trillion in revenue and employed 14.8 million people in fiscal year 2021, demonstrating a significant impact on the U.S. economy. The report highlights the critical role of immigrant entrepreneurs in job creation and economic growth.

feed.title: Dow dives 800 points, S&P 500 posts worst week since January after inflation hits 40-year high
feed.summary: U.S. stocks fell sharply on Friday, with the Dow dropping 880 points and the S&P 500 completing its worst week since January, following a higher-than-expected inflation report for May. The consumer price index reached its highest level since 1981, showing an 8.6% year-over-year increase, fueling concerns about a potential recession and prompting fears of a more aggressive Federal Reserve. Tech stocks, banks, and cyclical stocks were particularly impacted as the 2-year Treasury yield surged above 3%.

feed.title: Buy Now Pay Later business model faces test as rates rise
feed.summary: The Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) sector, which saw rapid growth during the pandemic, is now facing significant challenges due to reduced consumer spending, rising interest rates, and increased funding costs. This environment is expected to lead to consolidation within the industry, with smaller players struggling to compete against larger firms and new entrants like Apple. Regulatory scrutiny is also increasing globally, further impacting the business model.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Appoints Channel Leader for EMEA, LATAM
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has appointed Patricia Murphy as its new VP ecosystems, partners and strategic alliances for EMEA and LATAM. In this role, Murphy will lead the company’s channel across these regions and serve as a key member of CEO Helmut Reisinger’s executive team. She brings over 25 years of technology industry experience and will focus on driving engagement with partners, including MSSPs, to enhance Palo Alto Networks' market position.

feed.title: Trudeau meets Google boss Sundar Pichai, but no word on whether the tech CEO chewed out the PM over Bill C-18
feed.summary: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau met with Sundar Pichai, CEO of Alphabet (Google's parent company), but it's unclear if Bill C-18, a controversial Canadian media compensation bill, was discussed. The bill, criticized by experts like Michael Geist as a "shakedown," aims to make platforms like Google pay for linking to news content, giving the CRTC greater oversight. Google's Canadian head of public policy, Colin McKay, has stated that while Google supports journalism funding, Bill C-18 "would fundamentally and negatively impact millions of Canadians."

feed.title: Metaverse Stock Slump Might Be Big Opportunity
feed.summary: Technology stocks, especially those related to the metaverse, are currently underperforming, but some analysts see this as a potential opportunity. The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) is positioned to benefit if these stocks rebound, with Citi analysts highlighting several key holdings like Meta Platforms, Nvidia, and Apple as having significant metaverse exposure. Citi forecasts the metaverse and Web3 could be worth $13 trillion, suggesting long-term potential for patient investors in this themed ETF.

feed.title: Apple’s New CarPlay Is the Foreshock to Releasing Its Own Vehicle
feed.summary: Apple's redesigned CarPlay interface, unveiled at WWDC 2022, is seen as a precursor to the company eventually releasing its own vehicle. The article also touches on other Apple developments from WWDC 2022, including its new multitasking system, the absence of tvOS, and hints about an upcoming AR/VR headset. This reflects a broader expectation that Apple is laying the groundwork for a future in automotive technology.

feed.title: Google Engineer Claims AI System is Sentient; China's Tech Giants Flourish as Crackdown Eases
feed.summary: A Google engineer has been suspended after claiming that the company's LaMDA chatbot system shows signs of sentience, which Google denies, citing confidentiality breaches. Meanwhile, China's major tech companies like Baidu, Tencent, and Alibaba are experiencing a surge in share value as Beijing eases its regulatory crackdown on the sector. Twitter has also introduced a new "people-first" content moderation process to improve reporting of offensive content, especially amid Elon Musk's previous concerns about fake accounts.

feed.title: Grassroots mojo and 4 other reasons Starbucks workers have been so successful unionizing
feed.summary: Starbucks Workers United has achieved significant success in unionizing stores across the U.S., winning over 100 elections since December 2021. This unexpected success is attributed to a shift in public sentiment towards unions, a replicable worker-driven organizing model, Starbucks' progressive brand attracting pro-union employees, a strong sense of community among baristas, and a decentralized, grassroots approach to organizing. This movement could signal a broader union revival in the service sector.

feed.title: Newmark Completes Sale of Moffett Green, Fully-Leased Trophy Campus in Silicon Valley
feed.summary: Newmark has successfully completed the sale of Moffett Green, a 719,037 square-foot, fully-leased trophy office campus in Sunnyvale, California, to CommonWealth Partners, LLC. The transaction, which represents the largest single-asset investment office sale in Silicon Valley history, involved Meta Platforms, Inc. as the long-term tenant. Steven Golubchik and his team at Newmark facilitated the deal, highlighting continued investor demand for prime assets in high-barrier-to-entry markets and the strong performance of Moffett Park.

feed.title: China COVID controls makes Apple supplier Pegatron "emphasise" expansion elsewhere
feed.summary: Apple supplier Pegatron Corp is emphasizing expansion in countries other than China due to recent COVID-19 lockdowns in China, which caused plant suspensions and continue to lead to labor shortages. The company, an iPhone assembler, plans to expand its presence in Vietnam, India, Indonesia, and North America to diversify its production capacity and address staffing issues. This move also reflects a broader industry trend among customers to reduce concentration in Chinese manufacturing hubs like Shanghai and Suzhou.

feed.title: Police surveillance company seeks to acquire notorious Israeli NSO Group
feed.summary: L3 Harris, an American defense contractor previously known for its Stingray surveillance technology, is reportedly seeking to acquire the Israeli NSO Group, creator of the powerful and controversial Pegasus spyware. Privacy experts and civil liberties advocates express significant concern that this acquisition could lead to the proliferation of NSO Group's technology, which has been linked to human rights abuses globally, into local U.S. police departments. Critics highlight NSO Group's blacklisting by the U.S. government and the potential for increased misuse of surveillance tools against citizens, journalists, and activists.

feed.title: Far From a Couch Potato: How Shawn Nelson is Revolutionizing Your Living Room
feed.summary: Shawn Nelson, founder of Lovesac, aims to revolutionize consumer buying habits by designing durable, reconfigurable furniture like the Sactional, which he insists customers should keep for decades rather than frequently replacing. Despite leading a billion-dollar company, Nelson's personal "point" has evolved to prioritize his family and inspire consumers to buy less and buy better, challenging the norms of capitalism. He plans to expand this "Design for Life" ethos beyond furniture to other household items.

feed.title: Sonos tips: how to stereo pair, connect to a TV, and more
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to getting the most out of a Sonos system, covering frequently asked questions and offering 25 tips and tricks. It details aspects like Bluetooth connectivity, using Sonos without Wi-Fi, connecting to a TV, app usage, waterproofing, stereo pairing, and enhancing sound quality with Trueplay and external DACs. The guide also touches on streaming services, voice control, and integrating vinyl with Sonos, demonstrating the system's versatility.

feed.title: L3Harris in talks to buy Israeli spyware firm NSO - reports
feed.summary: U.S. defence contractor L3Harris is reportedly in talks to acquire Israeli spyware firm NSO Group, a deal that would require approvals from U.S. and Israeli authorities and L3Harris's board. The potential acquisition raises concerns in the White House due to NSO's controversial Pegasus software, which has been used by governments to spy on individuals, leading to the company being blacklisted by the U.S. Commerce Department. If approved, the deal could potentially remove NSO from the banned list and allow its assets to be exclusively used with the United States and its allies.

feed.title: How HP went from takeover bait to one of Warren Buffett’s top tech bets
feed.summary: Once considered takeover bait after its 2015 split, HP Inc. has seen a remarkable turnaround, becoming one of Warren Buffett's top tech investments. CEO Enrique Lores is driving this revival by diversifying into new high-growth markets like gaming and hybrid work and emphasizing sustainability, which has contributed significantly to sales. Despite the company's strong performance, Lores acknowledges that pandemic-driven growth is not sustainable and aims for steady growth from a higher baseline.

feed.title: Qualcomm’s Antitrust Fine for Payments to Apple Is Annulled by European Court
feed.summary: The European Union's second-highest court has annulled a €997 million ($1.04 billion) antitrust fine previously imposed on Qualcomm Inc. The fine was related to payments Qualcomm made to Apple Inc. for the exclusive use of Qualcomm chips in iPhones. The court's decision cited procedural irregularities by the European Commission in its case against Qualcomm, rather than substantive competition law violations.

feed.title: Ariana Grande Debuts Chapter 3 of R.E.M. Beauty with a Bang(s)
feed.summary: Ariana Grande's R.E.M. Beauty launched its third collection, 'Chapter 3: on your collar,' focusing on lip products. This collection, which includes five new formulas like lip oil, balm, liner, and lipsticks, follows the brand's 'drops' model and continues its extraterrestrial aesthetic. R.E.M. Beauty's initial collections, Chapters 1 and 2, are also available at Ulta stores.

feed.title: Garmin native running power on Forerunner 955 & 255 explained
feed.summary: Garmin has introduced native running power support for its new Forerunner 955 and 255 models, a move long awaited by users. While not a standalone solution, it integrates the existing Garmin Running Power app functionality more seamlessly, requiring an accessory like the HRM Pro/Run/Tri or Running Dynamics Pod. The article explains running power, how to set it up on compatible Garmin watches, and notes that Garmin's calculations are proprietary and not comparable to other brands like Stryd.

feed.title: Opinion | Semiconductor Dependency Imperils American Security
feed.summary: The article argues that the U.S.'s heavy reliance on Taiwan for advanced semiconductor manufacturing poses a significant national security risk, particularly in light of potential Chinese dominance. While some question the effectiveness of U.S. subsidies to foster domestic chip leadership, the authors emphasize that reducing dependence on TSMC is crucial to protect America's technology sector and military capabilities. A disruption in Taiwan's chip production could severely impact the U.S.

feed.title: BITI Chart | ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF
feed.summary: This page provides a live, interactive chart for the ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI), allowing users to analyze its performance. The chart offers various customization options and technical indicators to help investors understand price movements. It also includes current market data for BITI, such as its price, daily range, and 52-week range.

feed.title: Report: EU plans to investigate Broadcom’s proposed acquisition of VMware
feed.summary: The European Union is preparing to launch a lengthy "phase 2" antitrust investigation into Broadcom's proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware. This investigation could last over a year and potentially derail the deal, as multiple organizations, including VMware customer trade groups, have expressed concerns about potential price increases or forced bundling of Broadcom services. Broadcom is reportedly in preliminary discussions with EU officials to argue that the acquisition will not reduce competition or harm customers.

feed.title: Toronto FC ink ten-year BMO shirt sponsorship and naming rights renewal
feed.summary: Toronto FC has secured a ten-year extension of its foundational partnership with the Bank of Montreal (BMO), ensuring BMO remains the main shirt sponsor and retains naming rights for BMO Field. This renewal emphasizes a continued commitment to grassroots soccer initiatives, including expanding the Kickstart program to include girls for the first time. The agreement reinforces the long-standing collaboration between the MLS club and the financial group, especially timely after BMO Field's recent selection as a host venue for the 2026 Fifa World Cup.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Ties Exec Pay to Climate Targets
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced that a portion of its top executives' bonus pay will be linked to the management of value chain emissions, alongside mandatory climate training for leadership. This move is part of HPE's broader ESG initiatives, including accelerating its net-zero goal to 2040 and setting ambitious 2030 emissions reduction targets approved by the Science-based Target Initiative (SBTi). HPE is joining a growing trend of companies connecting executive compensation to ESG performance to drive sustainability.
